# Test binary analyzer functionality
#
# This test verifies that cmd2mcpserver can analyze Go binaries

# Build a test program
-- analyzer_test/main.go --
// Package main provides a test CLI tool for analysis
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
	"strings"
)

var (
	name = flag.String("name", "world", "name to greet")
	loud = flag.Bool("loud", false, "shout the greeting")
)

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	msg := fmt.Sprintf("Hello, %s!", *name)
	if *loud {
		msg = strings.ToUpper(msg)
	}
	fmt.Println(msg)
}

-- analyzer_test/go.mod --
module github.com/test/analyzer_test

go 1.21

-- analyzer_test/main_test.go --
package main

import "testing"

func TestGreeting(t *testing.T) {
	// Dummy test
}

# Build the binary
cd analyzer_test
exec go build -o test_binary

# Test cmd2mcpserver with verbose output on the binary
exec go run ../../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -v -tool-def test_binary
stdout 'Binary Analysis:'
stdout 'Module: github.com/test/analyzer_test'

# Test that automatic source detection works
exec go run ../../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -v test_binary
stdout 'Using source directory from binary analysis'
stdout 'Extracted 2 flag definitions'
